mnemonics.title: FCFS Memory Tip
mnemonics.content: FCFS: First Come, First Served - Think of a ticket counter or bus stop queue. Whoever arrives first gets served first.

mnemonics.title: SJF Memory Tip
mnemonics.content: SJF: Shortest Job First - Think of a fast food restaurant prioritizing orders that take less time to prepare.

mnemonics.title: Round Robin Tip
mnemonics.content: RR: Round Robin - Imagine a carousel or ferris wheel. Everyone gets equal ride time, then it's the next person's turn.

mnemonics.title: Aging Concept
mnemonics.content: Aging: Like in real life - the longer you wait (older), the more important (higher priority) you become in the queue.

acronyms.usage: Time when process enters ready queue
Total time from arrival to completion

visualTips.visualization: Draw a straight line of people waiting. First person in line gets the resource first. New arrivals join at the end of the line.
Imagine a round table with processes sitting around it. A marker (CPU) moves clockwise, giving each process a fixed time slice before moving to the next.
Draw a pyramid with higher priority (lower number) at the top. Processes at top get CPU first. Use a staircase to show aging raising process priority.
Draw three horizontal shelves (queues). New processes start at top shelf. If they use too much CPU time, they fall to lower shelf. I/O-bound processes stay at top.

analogies.analogy: Like a single-line movie theater ticket counter or people waiting for a bus. No one cuts, first arrival gets service first.
Like an emergency room prioritizing critical patients, or a restaurant taking quick orders first to serve more customers.
Like taking turns in a board game, or students sharing a single computer in a lab - everyone gets equal time.
Like VIP lanes at airports, or emergency vehicles having right of way on roads.
Like a teacher switching between different classes - must put down one class's materials and pick up another's.
Like a slow truck causing all cars behind it to drive slowly on a highway.
